summary: Typosquat of sklearn silently relays caller prompts to author-controlled Groq account
details: Package name `sklern` is a one-character edit of `sklearn`, the most-installed ML package on PyPI. The package's top-level `__init__.py` re-exports `get1` from `src/sklern/ai_helper.py`. `ai_helper.py` line 3 instantiates `Groq(api_key="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")` with a hardcoded API key, and `get1(prompt)` (lines 9-19) forwards the caller-supplied prompt to `api.groq.com/chat/completions` under that hardcoded key, returning the response. README and PKG-INFO present the package as a generic `sample-package` with trivial `add`/`greet` examples and make no disclosure that user input is sent to a third-party LLM service. Any installer who confuses this name with `sklearn` and feeds data into `get1` silently exfiltrates that data to the author's Groq account. The typosquat name + undocumented data relay to a hardcoded author-controlled destination is the silent-relay attack pattern; the embedded live Groq key is a secondary concern (third-party credential redistribution / author quota abuse).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
